channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Nippon, US Steel file suit after Biden administration blocks $15 billion deal
item.description: Nippon Steel and U.S. Steel are filing a federal lawsuit challenging the Biden administration’s decision to block a proposed nearly $15 billion deal for Nippon to acquire Pittsburgh-based U.S. Steel.
item.dc:creator: Fatima Hussein, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Biden blocks some new offshore drilling ahead of Trump takeover
item.description: Biden has announced that he’ll block new drilling off the entire East Coast, as well as California, Oregon and Washington state.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Frazin, NewsNation

item.title: Jimmy Carter raised climate change concerns 35 years before the Paris Accords
item.description: Jimmy Carter considered himself such an environmentalist that he made green his signature campaign color.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Legacy of Jan. 6 prosecutions in flux with Trump presidency on horizon
item.description: The Justice Department’s prosecution of the Jan. 6, 2021, Capitol attack — hailed as one of the “largest, most complex, and most resource-intensive investigations” in its history — could soon go out with a fizzle.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Gingrich warns Freedom Caucus to study his era as conservatives issue demand letter following Johnson vote
item.description: Newt Gingrich engineered the Contract With America after forming the Conservative Opportunity Society, which some compare to today's Freedom Caucus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Sales of Minnesota blackout license plates exceed 250,000 in first year
item.description: More than a quarter-million vehicles in the state now have the plates featuring a simple design of white letters and numbers on a black background.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Krueger, MPR News

item.title: Who is Giorgia Meloni? Trump hosts Italian PM at Mar-a-Lago
item.description: Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni is firebrand politician who is likely Trump's closest ideological ally in Europe.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Lawmakers brace for Trump's promised Jan. 6 pardons. Some are urging restraint
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The fourth anniversary of the Jan. 6 attack on the Capitol has a new focus as lawmakers brace for the prospect that President-elect Donald Trump may soon pardon many of the more than 1,500 people charged with crimes for their actions related to the riot.
item.dc:creator: KEVIN FREKING, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No Jan. 6 disruptions are expected as Trump's win boosts Republicans' faith in elections -- for now
item.description: This Jan. 6 won't be the same. Four years ago, then-President Donald Trump urged supporters to head to the Capitol to protest Congress' certification of Democrat Joe Biden's victory in the 2020 election. “Will be wild!” Trump promised on Twitter a few weeks before Jan. 6, 2021. And it was.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Melania Trump, Amazon partner to release new film about her life in theaters worldwide
item.description: Amazon Prime has exclusively licensed a documentary film that will give viewers an “unprecedented behind-the-scenes look" at First Lady Melania Trump, Fox News Digital has learned.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden's decision to block Nippon Steel takeover creates uncertainty for U.S. Steel workers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — By blocking a Japanese company’s takeover of U.S. Steel, President Joe Biden said he was protecting good jobs in the American heartland. He may be putting them at risk instead.
item.dc:creator: PAUL WISEMAN and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge denies Jan. 6 rioter’s bid to attend Trump inauguration
item.description: Judge Royce Lamberth denied the request in a Friday court order, reflecting on the event that took place after the 2020 presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: German leader is more worried about Musk's backing of a far-right party than his insults
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — German Chancellor Olaf Scholz says he's staying “cool” against critical personal comments made by Elon Musk but finds it worrying that the U.S. billionaire makes the effort to get involved in a general election by endorsing the far-right Alternative for Germany (AfD) party.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jimmy Carter's state funeral starts Saturday. Here is what to know
item.description: Six days of funeral observances for former President Jimmy Carter begin Saturday in Georgia, where he died on Dec. 29 at the age of 100.
item.dc:creator: Bill Barrow, WFLA, NBC

item.title: House Speaker Mike Johnson held onto his job, but there are signs of trouble ahead
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — This time was supposed to be different. But while House Speaker Mike Johnson on Friday avoided the dayslong ordeal that his predecessor, Kevin McCarthy, endured to become speaker, his relatively swift victory was hardly a unifying moment.
item.dc:creator: STEPHEN GROVES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Johnson faces tough road on Trump agenda after dramatic Speaker vote
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) kept his gavel amid a threat from the right flank of the House GOP, but managing the House GOP is not going to get any easier from here.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden blocks $14 billion acquisition of US Steel by Japan's Nippon Steel
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden has blocked the nearly $15 billion proposed deal for Nippon Steel of Japan to purchase Pittsburgh-based U.S. Steel — affirming his earlier vow on the presidential campaign trail to prevent the acquisition of Steeltown USA’s most storied steel company.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, JOSH BOAK and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Nathan Fletcher's accuser dismisses sexual assault allegations from lawsuit
item.description: The former Metropolitan Transit System employee who accused Nathan Fletcher of sexual misconduct in a bombshell lawsuit that propelled his resignation from office has walked back some of her most serious allegations in new court filings.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, Fox 5

item.title: Johnson battles for the Speakership: 5 things to watch
item.description: Friday’s Speaker vote is shaping up to be a nail-biter as Rep. Mike Johnson (R-La.), the current House leader, is scrambling to keep the gavel for the next two years in the face of conservative critics furious with his leadership style.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Here’s what Congress’s first day will look like
item.description: The 119th Congress kicks off Friday as lawmakers return to Washington with a long January to-do list and a packed first day. Confirmation hearings for President-elect Trump’s Cabinet nominees are set to get rolling in the coming weeks, and Trump himself will be inaugurated in less than three weeks.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Borrowers cheer Biden's record-setting student loan forgiveness, but left bitter with the system
item.description: While grateful to see their loan balance disappear, borrowers who received forgiveness under Biden lament a system they say is holding back millions of others.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to hold DC rally day before inauguration
item.description: The president-elect has not held a rally in the District of Columbia since he delivered a speech on the Ellipse on Jan. 6, 2021, which preceded a violent attack on the Capitol by supporters attempting to stop the certification of the 2020 election results.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Speaker race, Trump's second term, Carter funeral: January brings flurry of political events
item.description: New Year's Day ushers in a month of political events and milestones the likes of which Washington hasn't seen in a while.
item.dc:creator: Katie Wadington, NewsNation

item.title: All Democrats expected to participate in Speaker’s vote
item.description: All Democrats are expected to be in the Capitol on Friday for the vote to seat the next Speaker of the House, according to the Democratic whip’s office.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: New law takes effect to protect first responders
item.description: Several new laws go into effect for the 2025 New Year, and one of them is designed to protect first responders from threats.
item.dc:creator: Alessandra Young, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump calls it the 'center of the universe.' Mar-a-Lago is a magnet for those seeking influence
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump calls his Mar-a-Lago resort the "center of the universe.” Famous figures such as Elon Musk, Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau and Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg pop up at events held daily at the opulent club.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Court rejects Trump appeal in E. Jean Carroll sexual abuse and defamation case
item.description: Trump contended the trial court judge erred in several rulings — including decisions to allow the testimony of two women who alleged that Trump sexually assaulted them in the past.
item.dc:creator: Russell Lewis, MPR News

item.title: January special election called for tiebreaker Minnesota Senate seat after Dziedzic’s death
item.description: A special election has been set for late January to fill the Minnesota Senate seat of former Senate Majority Leader Kari Dziedzic who died last week.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News
